name: Al Green
abstract: Albert Leornes Greene (born April 13, 1946), known professionally as Al Green, is an American singer, songwriter, pastor and record producer. He is best known for recording a series of soul hit singles in the early 1970s, including "Tired of Being Alone" (1971), "I'm Still in Love with You" (1972), "Love and Happiness" (1973), "Take Me t…

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Al Green and Dianne Reeves
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Catherine Russell
about_quotes.evidence: One of the best, lately, I mean one of the best was singing behind Al Green. That was really fun I’ve got to say, I think it was on the David Letterman show.
about_quotes.doc_title: Catherine Russell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 26, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: or Al Green doing “Let’s Stay Together,” okay or Jimi Hendrix doing “Hey Joe,” the emotional response is still the same.
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: George "Tex" Stephens
about_quotes.evidence: when they're on the road with Al Green or if they're on the road with Big Joe Turner, they want to be seen drinking the best of everything

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Marshall
about_quotes.evidence: We’d do these shows with Stevie Wonder and Gladys Knight & the Pips and Wilson Pickett, Al Green, all of these really heavy soul acts.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Marshall interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Ran Blake
about_quotes.evidence: I think of Thelonious all the time, Abbey Lincoln, Billie Holiday, Al Green, Ray Charles... I might imagine Al Green doing [a melody]
about_quotes.doc_title: Ran Blake interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 16, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: Give me some Al Green. Give me some Memphis, give me some New Orleans.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007
